display.text:
# Component Install Scripts

> Automated installation scripts for OpenCode MCP servers and related tools. Provides idempotent setup with validation checkpoints.

---

## TABLE OF CONTENTS
<!-- ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->

1. [OVERVIEW](#1-overview)
2. [PREREQUISITES & QUICK START](#2-prerequisites--quick-start)
3. [INSTALLATION STRUCTURE](#3-installation-structure)
4. [FEATURES](#4-features)
5. [VERIFICATION & CONFIGURATION](#5-verification--configuration)
6. [USAGE EXAMPLES](#6-usage-examples)
7. [TROUBLESHOOTING](#7-troubleshooting)
8. [FAQ](#8-faq)
9. [RELATED DOCUMENTS](#9-related-documents)

<!-- /ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->

---

## 1. OVERVIEW
<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->

### What are Component Install Scripts?

These scripts automate installation and configuration of OpenCode components (MCP servers plus related tooling). Each script handles prerequisites, installation, configuration, and verification in a single command.

### Key Statistics

| Category | Count | Details |
|----------|-------|---------|
| Install Scripts | 9 | 3 real + 6 symlinks (component installers + 1 master installer) |
| Shared Utilities | 36 | Functions in `_utils.sh` |
| Platforms | 3 | macOS, Linux, Windows (WSL) |
| Install Time | 2-10 min | Per component, depending on complexity |

### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Idempotent** | Safe to run multiple times. Detects existing installations |
| **Validated** | Each phase has verification checkpoints |
| **Configurable** | Supports `--verbose`, `--skip-verify`, `--dry-run` flags |
| **Backup-Safe** | Creates timestamped backups before modifying configs |
| **Cross-Platform** | Works on macOS, Linux and Windows (WSL) |

### Requirements

| Requirement | Minimum | Recommended |
|-------------|---------|-------------|
| Node.js | 18+ | 20+ |
| npm | 9+ | 10+ |
| Bash | 4.0+ | 5.0+ |

<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->

---

## 2. PREREQUISITES & QUICK START
<!-- ANCHOR:quick-start -->

### Prerequisites

- Run from the project root so the relative script paths resolve.
- Ensure Bash, Node.js, and npm meet the requirements listed in [Overview](#1--overview).
- Make the installer scripts executable before the first run.

### 30-Second Setup

```bash
# 1. Navigate to project root
cd /path/to/your/project

# 2. Make scripts executable
chmod +x .opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/*.sh

# 3. Install core MCPs (recommended order)
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-code-mode.sh
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-spec-kit-memory.sh
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-sequential-thinking.sh
```

### Installation

The recommended installation path is to enable the three core MCP surfaces first, then layer on optional provider-specific installers such as Chrome DevTools.

### Verify Installation

```bash
# Check all MCPs are configured
cat opencode.json | python3 -m json.tool | grep -A2 '"mcp"'

# Expected: Shows configured MCP entries
```

### First Use

```bash
# Start OpenCode - MCPs load automatically
opencode

# Test Sequential Thinking
# Ask: "Use sequential thinking to analyze this problem..."
```

### Verification

- Confirm the expected MCP entries exist in `opencode.json`.
- Start OpenCode and verify the installed MCP surfaces appear without startup errors.
- If a component is still missing, continue with the script-specific checks in [Troubleshooting](#7--troubleshooting).

<!-- /ANCHOR:quick-start -->

---

## 3. INSTALLATION STRUCTURE
<!-- ANCHOR:structure -->

```
install_scripts/
├── _utils.sh                      # Shared utility functions (36 functions)
├── install-sequential-thinking.sh # Sequential Thinking MCP
├── install-spec-kit-memory.sh     # Spec Kit Memory MCP
├── install-code-mode.sh           # Code Mode MCP
├── install-chrome-devtools.sh     # Chrome DevTools MCP (bdg CLI)
├── install-all.sh                 # Master installer with --skip/--only flags
├── logs/                          # Installation logs
└── README.md                      # This file
```

### Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `_utils.sh` | 36 shared functions for logging, JSON, prerequisites |
| `install-all.sh` | Master installer with `--skip` and `--only` flags |

<!-- /ANCHOR:structure -->

---

## 4. FEATURES
<!-- ANCHOR:features -->

### Available Scripts

| Script | Component | Description | Prerequisites |
|--------|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| `install-sequential-thinking.sh` | Sequential Thinking MCP | Structured problem-solving via flexible thinking | Node.js 18+ |
| `install-spec-kit-memory.sh` | Spec Kit Memory MCP | Semantic vector search for conversation context | Node.js 20.11+, npm |
| `install-code-mode.sh` | Code Mode MCP | MCP orchestration via TypeScript execution | Node.js 18+ |
| `install-chrome-devtools.sh` | Chrome DevTools CLI | Browser debugging via CDP (bdg CLI) | Node.js 18+, Chrome |

### Shared Utilities (_utils.sh)

| Category | Functions | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| **Logging** | `_log`, `log_info`, `log_success`, `log_error`, `log_warn`, `log_step`, `log_debug` | Colored terminal output |
| **Prerequisites** | `check_command`, `check_node_version`, `check_npx`, `check_npm`, `check_jq`, `check_code_mode` | Dependency verification |
| **JSON** | `json_validate`, `json_has_key`, `json_set_value`, `json_add_mcp_entry`, `json_add_utcp_entry` | Config file manipulation |
| **Files** | `backup_file`, `find_project_root`, `get_project_root`, `ensure_dir`, `mcp_entry_exists`, `add_mcp_entry` | File operations |
| **User Input** | `confirm`, `confirm_action`, `prompt_value`, `prompt_secret`, `prompt_choice` | Interactive prompts |
| **Platform** | `detect_platform`, `detect_arch` | Environment detection |
| **Environment** | `env_add_var`, `env_has_var` | .env file management |
| **Verification** | `verify_command` | Command verification |
| **Help** | `show_header`, `show_help_footer` | Usage display |

### Master Installer (install-all.sh)

```bash
# Install all MCPs (run from project root)
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh

# Skip specific MCPs
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --skip chrome-devtools

# Install only specific MCPs
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --only code-mode --only mk-spec-memory

# Dry-run mode (preview without installing)
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --dry-run
```

<!-- /ANCHOR:features -->

---

## 5. VERIFICATION & CONFIGURATION
<!-- ANCHOR:configuration -->

### Configuration Files

**Location**: Project root

| File | Purpose | Modified By |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `opencode.json` | MCP server definitions | All scripts |
| `.utcp_config.json` | Code Mode provider configs | Code Mode |
| `.env` | API keys and secrets | Code Mode |
| `.env.example` | API key templates | Code Mode |

### Common Options

Verification and dry-run flags differ per script — they are not uniform:

| Option | Description | Supported by |
|--------|-------------|--------------|
| `-h, --help` | Show help message | All scripts |
| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose output | All scripts |
| `--skip-verify` | Skip verification step | `install-sequential-thinking.sh`, `install-spec-kit-memory.sh`, `install-code-mode.sh`, `install-chrome-devtools.sh` |
| `--no-verify` | Skip verification step (master installer name) | `install-all.sh` |
| `--dry-run` | Preview changes without making them | `install-all.sh`, `install-code-mode.sh` (not `install-sequential-thinking.sh`, `install-spec-kit-memory.sh`, `install-chrome-devtools.sh`) |

### Script-Specific Options

**install-chrome-devtools.sh**:
| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--force` | Force reinstallation |
| `--add-profile` | Add CHROME_PATH to shell profile |

<!-- /ANCHOR:configuration -->

---

## 6. USAGE EXAMPLES
<!-- ANCHOR:usage-examples -->

### Example 1: Install Core MCPs

```bash
# Install the three core MCPs in recommended order (run from project root)
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-code-mode.sh
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-spec-kit-memory.sh
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-sequential-thinking.sh
```

**Result**: Core MCPs configured in `opencode.json`, ready for use.

### Example 2: CI/CD Installation

```bash
# Dry-run to preview installation
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --dry-run

# Install specific MCPs only
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --only code-mode --only sequential-thinking

# Verbose mode to see all output
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh -v
```

**Result**: MCPs installed with appropriate flags for automation.

### Common Patterns

| Pattern | Command | When to Use |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Core setup | `.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --only code-mode --only mk-spec-memory --only sequential-thinking` | New project setup |
| Full setup | `.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh` | Complete MCP installation |
| Reinstall | `.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-<component>.sh --force` | Fix broken installation |
| Debug | `.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-<component>.sh -v` | Troubleshoot issues |

<!-- /ANCHOR:usage-examples -->

---

## 7. TROUBLESHOOTING
<!-- ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->

### Common Issues

#### Node.js not found

**Symptom**: `command not found: node`

**Cause**: Node.js not installed or not in PATH

**Solution**:
```bash
# Install Node.js 18+ from nodejs.org
# Or via nvm:
nvm install 18
nvm use 18
```

#### Permission denied

**Symptom**: `Permission denied` when running script

**Cause**: Script not executable

**Solution**:
```bash
chmod +x .opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/*.sh
```

#### opencode.json not found

**Symptom**: `Error: opencode.json not found`

**Cause**: Running from wrong directory

**Solution**:
```bash
# Run from project root containing opencode.json
cd /path/to/your/project
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-<component>.sh
```

#### JSON validation failed

**Symptom**: `Error: JSON validation failed`

**Cause**: Syntax error in config file

**Solution**:
```bash
# Check JSON syntax
python3 -m json.tool < opencode.json

# Restore from backup if needed
cp opencode.json.bak.YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS opencode.json
```

### Quick Fixes

| Problem | Quick Fix |
|---------|-----------|
| Node.js not found | Install from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/) |
| npx not found | `npm install -g npx` |
| Chrome not found | Set `CHROME_PATH` environment variable |
| Code Mode not configured | Run `install-code-mode.sh` first |
| MCP not appearing | Restart OpenCode/Claude Code |

### Diagnostic Commands

```bash
# Check Node.js version
node --version

# Check npm version
npm --version

# Validate opencode.json
python3 -m json.tool < opencode.json

# Check MCP package accessibility
npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking --help

# Check bdg installation
bdg --version
```

<!-- /ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->

---

## 8. FAQ
<!-- ANCHOR:faq -->

### General Questions

**Q: What order should I install MCPs?**

A: Install Code Mode first (foundation). Otherwise, order doesn't matter. Recommended: Code Mode → Spec Kit Memory → Sequential Thinking → others.

---

**Q: Are the scripts safe to run multiple times?**

A: Yes. All scripts are idempotent. They detect existing installations and skip redundant work. Config files are backed up before modification.

---

**Q: Do I need all 5 component installers?**

A: No. Install based on your needs:
- **Core**: Code Mode MCP, Spec Kit Memory MCP, Sequential Thinking MCP
- **Optional**: Chrome DevTools CLI (debugging)

---

### Technical Questions

**Q: Where are backups stored?**

A: In the same directory as the original file, with timestamp suffix:
```
opencode.json.bak.20260101_120000
.utcp_config.json.bak.20260101_120000
```

---

**Q: How do I add a new MCP script?**

A: Follow the template pattern:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_utils.sh"

# Check prerequisites → Install → Configure → Verify
```

See the Contributing section in the source for the full template.

---

**Q: Can I run these in Docker/CI?**

A: Yes. Use `--dry-run` to preview changes without installing:
```bash
.opencode/install_guides/install_scripts/install-all.sh --dry-run
```

<!-- /ANCHOR:faq -->

---

## 9. RELATED DOCUMENTS
<!-- ANCHOR:related-documents -->

### Internal Documentation

| Document | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| [MCP - Code Mode.md](../MCP%20-%20Code%20Mode.md) | Code Mode installation guide |
| [MCP - Sequential Thinking.md](../MCP%20-%20Sequential%20Thinking.md) | Sequential Thinking guide |
| [MCP - Spec Kit Memory.md](../MCP%20-%20Spec%20Kit%20Memory.md) | Spec Kit Memory guide |
| [MCP - Chrome Dev Tools.md](../MCP%20-%20Chrome%20Dev%20Tools.md) | Chrome DevTools guide |

### External Resources

| Resource | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) | Official MCP documentation |
| [OpenCode](https://github.com/sst/opencode) | OpenCode CLI documentation |
| [Code Mode](https://github.com/universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode) | UTCP Code Mode |

---

*Part of the [OpenCode Development Environment](https://github.com/MichelKerkmeester/opencode-dev-environment)*

<!-- /ANCHOR:related-documents -->

ENVIRONMENT DETECTION 169: 170: Answer these questions to configure your installation: 171: 172: ### Q1: Platform 173: - **macOS** → Full support, Homebrew for dependencies 174: - **Linux** → Full support, apt/dnf for dependencies 175: - **Windows WSL** → Full support via WSL2, follow Linux instructions 176: 177: ### Q2: Installation Scope 178: - **Project-specific** (recommended) → Install in `.opencode/` directory 179: - **Global** → Install in user home directory 180: 181: ### Q3: LLM Provider 182: - **Claude (Anthropic)** → Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` 183: - **GitHub Copilot** → Requires GitHub authentication 184: - **OpenAI / Codex** → Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` 185: > **Note:** Spec Kit Memory embeddings use a local-first `auto` cascade: Ollama (default, local daemon when reachable), HF Local (pure-Node local fallback), Voyage (cloud, opt-in via `VOYAGE_API_KEY`), and OpenAI (cloud, opt-in via `OPENAI_API_KEY`). The default `auto` cascade works out of the box with no API key. See [Section 10.2](#102-spec-kit-memory-context-preservation) for details. 186: 187: ### Windows-Specific Configuration 188: 189:
190: Path Variables 191: 192: The `opencode.json` configuration uses `${HOME}` for portable paths. On Windows: 193: 194: 1. **PowerShell**: `${HOME}` works natively 195: 2. **CMD**: Replace `${HOME}` with your actual home path (e.g., `C:/Users/YourName`) 196: 3. **Git Bash**: `${HOME}` works natively 197: 198: If you encounter path issues, manually replace `${HOME}` in `opencode.json` with your full path. 199: 200:

Or run: `npm install --global windows-build-tools` (requires admin) 225: 226: This is needed for: 227: - `better-sqlite3` (Memory MCP server) 228: - `sqlite-vec` (Vector search extension) 229: 230:

Run: 241: ```bash 242: # Fix line endings (Git Bash or WSL) 243: dos2unix script.sh 244: 245: # Or use git to reset 246: git checkout -- script.sh 247: ``` 248: 249:

PHASE 2: LOCAL EMBEDDINGS 485: 486: Spec Kit Memory resolves the active local embedding profile automatically. The HF Local ONNX profile runs on Node.js without external services. When an Ollama daemon is reachable on `localhost:11434`, the cascade can promote to Ollama. 487: 488: No separate local model service is required for Memory MCP embeddings. Continue to Phase 3 for MCP server setup. 489: 490: --- 491: 492: 493: 494: ## 10. PHASE 3: MCP SERVERS 495: 496: > **Skip Check:** Run `grep -q '"code_mode"' opencode.json && grep -q '"mk-spec-memory"' opencode.json && echo "✅ All configured"`. If all configured, skip to Phase 4. 497: 498: ### Installation Order (Important!) 499: 500: 1. **Code Mode** (foundation, install FIRST) 501: 2. Spec Kit Memory (context preservation, **now supports multiple embedding providers**) 502: 3. Sequential Thinking (complex reasoning) 503: 504: --- 505: 506: ### 10.1 Code Mode (Foundation) 507: 508: Code Mode provides TypeScript execution environment for all external MCP tools. 509: 510: > **Detailed Guide:** See [MCP - Code Mode.md](./MCP%20-%20Code%20Mode.md) for comprehensive configuration and provider setup. 511: 512: **Check:** `npx utcp-mcp --help >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Installed"` → If "Installed" shown, skip to config 513: 514: **Install if missing:** 515: ```bash 516: # Global install for CLI access 517: npm install -g utcp-mcp 518: ``` 519: 520: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 521: ```json 522: { 523: "mcp": { 524: "code_mode": { 525: "command": "npx", 526: "args": ["utcp-mcp"], 527: "env": {} 528: } 529: } 530: } 531: ``` 532: 533: **Create `.utcp_config.json` in project root:** 534: ```json 535: { 536: "manuals": [] 537: } 538: ``` 539: 540: **Code Mode Providers (External Tools):** 541: 542: Code Mode enables access to external MCP tools. Each provider has its own detailed install guide: 543: 544: | Provider | Tools | Install Guide | 545: |----------|-------|---------------| 546: | **Chrome DevTools** | 26 | [MCP - Chrome Dev Tools.md](./MCP%20-%20Chrome%20Dev%20Tools.md) - Browser debugging (MCP mode) | 547: | **GitHub** | 26 | [MCP - Code Mode.md](./MCP%20-%20Code%20Mode.md) - Repository operations, issues, PRs | 548: | **Your CMS** (e.g., a CMS-vendor MCP server) | varies | [MCP - Code Mode.md](./MCP%20-%20Code%20Mode.md) - CMS management, site operations | 549: 550: > **Note**: All Code Mode providers are configured in `.utcp_config.json`, NOT `opencode.json`. See each provider's install guide for configuration details. 551: 552: ### Validation: `code_mode_check` 553: 554: - [ ] npx utcp-mcp responds to --help 555: - [ ] .utcp_config.json exists in project root 556: 557: **Quick Verification:** 558: ```bash 559: npx utcp-mcp --help >/dev/null 2>&1 && test -f .utcp_config.json && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 560: ``` 561: 562: --- 563: 564: ### 10.2 Spec Kit Memory (Context Preservation) 565: 566: Spec Kit Memory provides conversation context preservation with vector search. 567: 568: > **Detailed Guide:** See [MCP - Spec Kit Memory.md](./MCP%20-%20Spec%20Kit%20Memory.md) for comprehensive configuration and troubleshooting. 569: 570: **V12.0: Multiple Embedding Providers** 571: 572: Spec Kit Memory now supports four providers in cascade: 573: 574: | Provider | When to use | Dimension | Requirements | 575: |----------|-------------|-----------|------------| 576: | **Ollama** | Local default (daemon) | 768 | Ollama on `localhost:11434` | 577: | **HF Local** | Local fallback, Node.js only | 768 | Node.js only | 578: | **OpenAI** | Cloud opt-in | 1536/3072 | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | 579: | **Voyage** | Cloud opt-in | 1024 | `VOYAGE_API_KEY` | 580: 581: **Default provider:** `auto`. The active default is **Ollama** when its local daemon is reachable; otherwise the cascade falls back to local **HF Local** (pure-Node), then to opt-in cloud. Cloud (OpenAI/Voyage) is never auto-selected silently — it requires an explicit key or `EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER`. 582: 583: **Provider selection (local-first cascade order when `EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=auto` or unset):** 584: 1. Explicit `EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER` env var (if set and not `auto`) 585: 2. **Ollama** (default) if the local daemon is reachable on `localhost:11434` 586: 3. **HF Local** (pure-Node local fallback) when Ollama is unavailable 587: 4. **OpenAI** when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set (cloud, opt-in) 588: 5. **Voyage** when `VOYAGE_API_KEY` is set (cloud, opt-in) 589: - Manual override: `export EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=ollama|hf-local|voyage|openai|auto` 590: 591: **Location:** Bundled in project at `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/` 592: 593: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 594: ```json 595: { 596: "mcp": { 597: "mk-spec-memory": { 598: "command": "node", 599: "args": [".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"], 600: "env": { 601: "EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER": "auto" 602: } 603: } 604: } 605: } 606: ``` 607: 608: **Optional environment variables:** 609: ```bash 610: # Provider selection (ollama|hf-local|voyage|openai|auto) 611: export EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=auto # Default: auto-cascade provider selection 612: 613: # Voyage config (cloud opt-in) 614: export VOYAGE_API_KEY=pa-... 615: export VOYAGE_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL=voyage-4 # Default 616: 617: # OpenAI config (if using OpenAI) 618: export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... 619: export OPENAI_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small # Default 620: 621: # HF Local config (pure-Node local fallback provider; nomic-only menu) 622: export HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL=nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 # Default 623: export HF_EMBEDDINGS_DTYPE=q8 # Default (also: fp32, fp16, q4, int8, uint8, bnb4) 624: 625: # Ollama config (local daemon opt-in) 626: export OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL=nomic-embed-text-v1.5 # Default 627: 628: # Database directory (optional - default: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/) 629: export MEMORY_DB_DIR=/path/to/database 630: ``` 631: 632: **Note on per-profile DB:** 633: Each provider+model+dimension combination uses its own SQLite database. This prevents "dimension mismatch" errors and allows switching providers without migrations. 634: 635: **Initialize database:** 636: ```bash 637: # The database is created automatically on first run 638: # Verify the directory exists 639: ls -la .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/ 640: ``` 641: 642: ### Validation: `mk_spec_memory_check` 643: 644: - [ ] Context server JS file exists 645: - [ ] Database directory exists (or will be created) 646: - [ ] Embeddings provider loads on first run (auto-cascade: ollama (default, if reachable) -> hf-local (local fallback) -> `OPENAI_API_KEY` -> `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) 647: 648: **Quick Verification:** 649: ```bash 650: test -f .opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs && grep -q 'mk-spec-memory-launcher' opencode.json && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 651: ``` 652: 653: **Verify active provider:** 654: Use the `memory_health` tool after starting OpenCode to see which provider is active: 655: ```json 656: { 657: "embeddingProvider": { 658: "provider": "openai", 659: "model": "text-embedding-3-small", 660: "dimension": 1536, 661: "healthy": true 662: } 663: } 664: ``` 665: 666: --- 667: 668: ### 10.3 Sequential Thinking (Complex Reasoning) 669: 670: Sequential Thinking provides structured reasoning chains for complex problems. 671: 672: > **Detailed Guide:** See [MCP - Sequential Thinking.md](./MCP%20-%20Sequential%20Thinking.md) for comprehensive configuration and usage. 673: 674: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 675: ```json 676: { 677: "mcp": { 678: "sequential_thinking": { 679: "command": "npx", 680: "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"], 681: "env": {} 682: } 683: } 684: } 685: ``` 686: 687: ### Validation: `sequential_thinking_check` 688: 689: - [ ] npx can download and run the package 690: - [ ] Configuration added to opencode.json 691: 692: **Quick Verification:** 693: ```bash 694: grep -q '"sequential_thinking"' opencode.json && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 695: ``` 696: 697: --- 698: 699: ### 10.4 System Code Graph (`mk_code_index` — Structural Code Intelligence) 700: 701: The standalone `mk_code_index` MCP server registers 8 tools for structural AST indexing, blast-radius analysis, neighborhood context, query classification and change detection (`code_graph_scan/query/context/status/verify/apply/classify_query_intent`, `detect_changes`). 702: 703: > **Detailed Guide:** See [system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../skills/system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) for full installation, configuration, verification, and troubleshooting. 704: > **Runtime Diagnostics:** See [SET-UP - Code Graph.md](./SET-UP%20-%20Code%20Graph.md) for `/doctor code-graph` post-install audit. 705: 706: **Location:** Bundled in this repository at `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/`. 707: 708: **Check:** 709: ```bash 710: test -f .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js && echo "Installed" || echo "Needs build" 711: ``` 712: 713: **Install if missing:** 714: ```bash 715: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph install 716: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run clean 717: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build 718: ``` 719: 720: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 721: ```json 722: { 723: "mcp": { 724: "mk_code_index": { 725: "type": "local", 726: "command": ["node", ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"], 727: "environment": { 728: "_NOTE_1_DB": "Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides.", 729: "_NOTE_2_TOOLS": "Registers 8 tools: code_graph_scan/query/context/status/verify/apply/classify_query_intent, detect_changes. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*", 730: "SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS": "false", 731: "SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS": "false", 732: "SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS": "false", 733: "SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS": "false", 734: "SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS": "false" 735: } 736: } 737: } 738: } 739: ``` 740: 741: **Maintainer-mode toggle:** End-user defaults keep all 5 `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags `false` so the index stays small. Maintainers who want full coverage set `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true` in `.env.local` (gitignored) and the launcher forces all 5 flags to `true` on startup. 742: 743: ### Validation: `mk_code_index_check` 744: 745: - [ ] Launcher exists: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` 746: - [ ] Built entry exists: `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` 747: - [ ] Configuration added to opencode.json (key: `mk_code_index`) 748: 749: **Quick Verification:** 750: ```bash 751: test -f .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs && \ 752: test -f .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js && \ 753: grep -q '"mk_code_index"' opencode.json && \ 754: echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 755: ``` 756: 757: --- 758: 759: ### 10.5 Skill Advisor (`mk_skill_advisor` — Native Recommendation) 760: 761: The standalone `mk_skill_advisor` MCP server registers 8 tools (`advisor_recommend/rebuild/status/validate`, `skill_graph_scan/query/status/validate`) for prompt-time skill recommendation and skill-graph queries. 762: 763: > **Detailed Guide:** See [system-skill-advisor/INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../skills/system-skill-advisor/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) for full installation, configuration, rollback, and operator notes. 764: > **Runtime Tuning:** See [SET-UP - Skill Advisor.md](./SET-UP%20-%20Skill%20Advisor.md) for post-install adjustments. 765: 766: **Location:** Bundled in this repository at `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/`. 767: 768: **Check:** 769: ```bash 770: test -f .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/mcp_server/advisor-server.js && echo "Installed" || echo "Needs build" 771: ``` 772: 773: **Install if missing:** 774: ```bash 775: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server install 776: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server run build 777: ``` 778: 779: > **Build pipeline note:** `npm run build` builds `@spec-kit/shared`, compiles advisor hooks and MCP server code, then copies `data/*.json` into `dist/mcp_server/data`. Use `bash .opencode/scripts/copy-skill-advisor-dist-data.sh` only as a manual repair helper when dist data is missing or stale. 780: 781: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 782: ```json 783: { 784: "mcp": { 785: "mk_skill_advisor": { 786: "type": "local", 787: "command": ["node", ".opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"], 788: "environment": { 789: "_NOTE_1_DB": "Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database/skill-graph.sqlite by default; MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR overrides.", 790: "_NOTE_2_TOOLS": "Registers 8 tools: advisor_recommend/rebuild/status/validate plus skill_graph_scan/query/status/validate. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_skill_advisor__*", 791: "MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR": ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database", 792: "MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT": "trusted", 793: "_NOTE_3_SHADOW_MODE": "SPECKIT_ADVISOR_SHADOW_MODE is currently inert: documented intent only, no runtime reader yet.", 794: "SPECKIT_ADVISOR_SHADOW_MODE": "0", 795: "SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED": "0" 796: } 797: } 798: } 799: } 800: ``` 801: 802: ### Validation: `mk_skill_advisor_check` 803: 804: - [ ] Launcher exists: `.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs` 805: - [ ] Built entry exists: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/mcp_server/advisor-server.js` 806: - [ ] Configuration added to opencode.json (key: `mk_skill_advisor`) 807: 808: **Quick Verification:** 809: ```bash 810: test -f .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs && \ 811: grep -q '"mk_skill_advisor"' opencode.json && \ 812: echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 813: ``` 814: 815: --- 816: 817: ### 10.6 Chrome DevTools CLI (Optional) 818: 819: Chrome DevTools provides browser automation and debugging via CLI. 820: 821: **Install:** 822: ```bash 823: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha 824: ``` 825: 826: **Usage (CLI-first approach):** 827: ```bash 828: # List available commands 829: bdg --list 830: 831: # Describe a specific command 832: bdg --describe screenshot 833: 834: # Take screenshot 835: bdg screenshot --url https://example.com --output screenshot.png 836: ``` 837: 838: ### Validation: `chrome_devtools_check` 839: 840: - [ ] bdg command responds to --list 841: - [ ] bdg --version shows version number 842: 843: **Quick Verification:** 844: ```bash 845: bdg --version >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 846: ``` 847: 848: --- 849: 850: ### Phase 3 Complete Validation: `mcp_servers_check` 851: 852: - [ ] Code Mode: npx utcp-mcp --version responds 853: - [ ] Spec Kit Memory (`mk-spec-memory`): configured in opencode.json 854: - [ ] Skill Advisor (`mk_skill_advisor`): configured in opencode.json 855: - [ ] System Code Graph (`mk_code_index`): configured in opencode.json 856: - [ ] Sequential Thinking: configured in opencode.json 857: - [ ] (Optional) Chrome DevTools: bdg --version responds 858: 859: **Quick Verification:** 860: ```bash 861: grep -q '"code_mode"' opencode.json && \ 862: grep -q '"mk-spec-memory"' opencode.json && \ 863: grep -q '"mk_skill_advisor"' opencode.json && \ 864: grep -q '"mk_code_index"' opencode.json && \ 865: grep -q '"sequential_thinking"' opencode.json && \ 866: echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 867: ``` 868: 869: --- 870: 871: 872: 873: ## 11. PHASE 4: PLUGINS 874: 875: ### 11.1 Native Skills (Built-in) 876: 877: OpenCode v1.0.190+ has **native skill support** built-in. No plugin installation required. 878: 879: Skills are automatically discovered from: 880: - `.opencode/skills//SKILL.md` (project-level) 881: - `~/.opencode/skills//SKILL.md` (global) 882: - `.claude/skills//SKILL.md` (Claude-compatible) 883: 884: **Current Skills:** 885: | Skill | Version | Purpose | 886: | -------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | 887: | mcp-code-mode | v1.0.7.0 | External tool orchestration | 888: | system-spec-kit | v2.2.26.0 | Spec folder + template system + context preservation | 889: | mcp-chrome-devtools | v1.0.7.0 | Browser debugging | 890: | cli-codex | v1.2.0 | OpenAI Codex CLI orchestration for code and research | 891: | cli-claude-code | v1.0.0 | Claude Code CLI orchestration | 892: | sk-code | v1.1.0.0 | Stack-aware code workflow + quality standard (customizable per project) | 893: | sk-code-review | v1.2.0.0 | Findings-first baseline code review standards | 894: | sk-doc | v1.1.2.0 | Unified markdown and skill management | 895: | sk-git | v1.1.0.0 | Git workflow orchestrator | 896: | sk-prompt | v1.2.0.0 | Prompt engineering frameworks and scoring | 897: 898: **How it works:** 899: - OpenCode scans skill folders on startup 900: - Skills are surfaced as `skills_*` functions (e.g., `skills_mcp_code_mode`) 901: - Agents read `SKILL.md` files directly when a task matches 902: 903: **No configuration needed.** Skills in `.opencode/skills/` are automatically available. 904: 905: ### Validation: `native_skills_check` 906: 907: - [ ] Skills directory exists 908: - [ ] At least one SKILL.md file present 909: - [ ] SKILL.md has required frontmatter (name, description) 910: 911: **Quick Verification:** 912: ```bash 913: test -d .opencode/skills && ls .opencode/skills/*/SKILL.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 914: ``` 915: 916: --- 917: 918: ### 11.2 Antigravity Auth (Google OAuth) 919: 920: Enables Google OAuth authentication for Claude. 921: 922: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 923: ```json 924: { 925: "plugins": [ 926: "opencode-antigravity-auth@1.2.2" 927: ] 928: } 929: ``` 930: 931: **Usage:** 932: - Plugin activates automatically when OpenCode starts 933: - Follow OAuth prompts in terminal when authentication is needed 934: 935: --- 936: 937: ### 11.3 OpenAI Codex Auth (ChatGPT OAuth) 938: 939: Enables ChatGPT OAuth authentication for OpenAI models. 940: 941: **Configure in `opencode.json`:** 942: ```json 943: { 944: "plugins": [ 945: "opencode-openai-codex-auth@4.1.1" 946: ] 947: } 948: ``` 949: 950: **Environment variables (optional):** 951: ```bash 952: export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-api-key" 953: ``` 954: 955: --- 956: 957: ### Phase 4 Complete Validation: `plugins_check` 958: 959: - [ ] Skills directory exists and contains skills 960: - [ ] (Optional) Auth plugins configured in opencode.json 961: 962: **Quick Verification:** 963: ```bash 964: test -d .opencode/skills && [ $(ls -1 .opencode/skills | wc -l) -ge 1 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL" 965: ``` 966: 967: --- 968: 969: 970: 971: ## 12. CONFIGURATION TEMPLATES 972: 973: ### 12.1 Complete `opencode.json` (Full Bundle) 974: 975: ```json 976: { 977: "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.schema.json", 978: "mcp": { 979: "code_mode": { 980: "command": "npx", 981: "args": ["utcp-mcp"], 982: "env": {} 983: }, 984: "mk-spec-memory": { 985: "command": "node", 986: "args": [".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"] 987: }, 988: "sequential_thinking": { 989: "command": "npx", 990: "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"], 991: "env": {} 992: } 993: }, 994: "plugins": [ 995: "opencode-antigravity-auth@1.2.2", 996: "opencode-openai-codex-auth@4.1.1" 997: ] 998: } 999: ``` 1000: 1001: ### 12.2 Complete `.utcp_config.json` 1002: 1003: ```json 1004: { 1005: "manuals": [] 1006: } 1007: ``` 1008: 1009: **Note:** External tools (GitHub, your CMS, etc.) are added to manuals array as needed. See Code Mode skill documentation for configuration examples. 1010: 1011: ### 12.3 Minimal Bundle Configuration 1012: 1013: **`opencode.json`:** 1014: ```json 1015: { 1016: "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.schema.json", 1017: "mcp": { 1018: "code_mode": { 1019: "command": "npx", 1020: "args": ["utcp-mcp"], 1021: "env": {} 1022: }, 1023: "mk-spec-memory": { 1024: "command": "node", 1025: "args": [".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"] 1026: } 1027: }, 1028: "plugins": [] 1029: } 1030: ``` 1031: 1032: **`.utcp_config.json`:** 1033: ```json 1034: { 1035: "manuals": [] 1036: } 1037: ``` 1038: 1039: --- 1040: 1041: 1042: 1043: ## 13. FINAL VERIFICATION 1044: 1045: ### Checklist 1046: 1047: - [ ] Prerequisites: Node.js 18+, Python 3.10+, uv 1048: - [ ] All native MCP servers configured in opencode.json 1049: - [ ] Skills directory exists with native skills 1050: - [ ] Configuration files exist and are valid JSON 1051: 1052: ### Quick Verification 1053: 1054: ```bash 1055: # Full verification one-liner 1056: node --version | grep -E "^v(1[89]|2[0-9])" && \ 1057: python3 --version | grep -E "3\.(1[0-9]|[2-9][0-9])" && \ 1058: test -f opencode.json && \ 1059: test -d .opencode/skills && \ 1060: echo "✅ INSTALLATION COMPLETE" || echo "❌ VERIFICATION FAILED" 1061: ``` 1062: 1063: ### Test Commands 1064: 1065: ```bash 1066: npx utcp-mcp --list-tools # Code Mode 1067: ls .opencode/skills/ # Skills 1068: cat opencode.json | jq '.mcp | keys' # MCP servers 1069: ``` 1070: 1071: ### Run `/doctor` for end-to-end health check (recommended) 1072: 1073: After the static checks above pass, run the interactive doctor surface to verify every subsystem ends up in a healthy state: 1074: 1075: ```text 1076: /doctor # Interactive menu — pick a subsystem or "Update everything to match latest release" 1077: /doctor:update # Full sweep across every database (8-25 min) 1078: /doctor:mcp debug # MCP-infra diagnostic only (~30s) 1079: ``` 1080: 1081: - `/doctor` opens an 11-option menu. **Option 1** is "Update everything to match latest spec-kit release" — the right pick after a fresh install (runs `/doctor:update --migrate`). 1082: - `/doctor:update` rebuilds every database in dependency-safe order with snapshots + auto-rollback. Use it after upgrades or large packet moves. 1083: - `/doctor:mcp debug` checks the native MCP servers (Spec Kit Memory, Skill Advisor, System Code Graph, Code Mode, Sequential Thinking) and offers guided repair with `--fix`. 1084: 1085: Full reference: `.opencode/commands/doctor/speckit.md` + `.opencode/commands/doctor/_routes.yaml`. Canonical subsystem targets: memory, causal-graph, code-graph, deep-loop, skill-advisor, skill-budget. 1086: 1087: --- 1088: 1089: 1090: 1091: ## 14. DISASTER RECOVERY 1092: 1093: Emergency procedures for backup, recovery and clean uninstallation of OpenCode components. Use this section when things go wrong or when performing maintenance. 1094: 1095: ### Quick Reference 1096: 1097: | Problem | Solution | Section | 1098: | -------------------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------- | 1099: | **Want to start fresh** | Clean Uninstall | [14.2](#142-uninstall-commands) | 1100: | **Installation failed** | Rollback | [14.3](#143-rollback) | 1101: | **MCP server stuck** | Kill processes | [14.4](#144-emergency-recovery-commands) | 1102: | **Database corrupted** | Rebuild database | [14.4](#144-emergency-recovery-commands) | 1103: | **Not sure what's broken** | Run health check | [14.5](#145-health-check) | 1104: | **About to make changes** | Backup first | [14.1](#141-backup--restore) | 1105: 1106: --- 1107: 1108: ### 14.1 Backup & Restore 1109: 1110: **Quick Commands:** 1111: 1112: ```bash 1113: # Backup 1114: BACKUP="$HOME/.opencode-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)" && mkdir -p "$BACKUP" && cp opencode.json .utcp_config.json "$BACKUP/" 2>/dev/null && cp -r .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database "$BACKUP/" 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Backed up to $BACKUP" 1115: 1116: # List backups 1117: ls -lhd ~/.opencode-backup-* 2>/dev/null || echo "No backups found" 1118: 1119: # Restore (replace BACKUP path) 1120: BACKUP="$HOME/.opencode-backup-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS" && cp "$BACKUP/opencode.json" "$BACKUP/.utcp_config.json" ./ 2>/dev/null && cp -r "$BACKUP/database" .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/ 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Restored" 1121: ``` 1122: 1123: --- 1124: 1125: ### 14.2 Uninstall Commands 1126: 1127: | Component | Uninstall Command | Notes | 1128: | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | 1129: | **Code Mode** | `npm uninstall -g utcp-mcp` | Remove from opencode.json + delete .utcp_config.json | 1130: | **Chrome DevTools CLI** | `npm uninstall -g browser-debugger-cli` | | 1131: | **Spec Kit Memory** | `rm .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/*.sqlite` | Database will be recreated | 1132: | **Sequential Thinking** | Remove from `opencode.json` | No files to delete | 1133: | **Skills** | `rm -rf .opencode/skills//` | Remove specific skill folder | 1134: | **All Skills** | `rm -rf .opencode/skills/` | Removes all skills | 1135: 1136: **To remove MCP server:** Edit `opencode.json` and delete the corresponding entry from the `mcp` object. 1137: 1138: --- 1139: 1140: ### 14.3 Rollback 1141: 1142: ```bash 1143: # Quick rollback to latest backup 1144: BACKUP=$(ls -td ~/.opencode-backup-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) && [ -n "$BACKUP" ] && cp "$BACKUP/opencode.json" "$BACKUP/.utcp_config.json" ./ 2>/dev/null && echo "✅ Rolled back to $BACKUP" || echo "❌ No backup found" 1145: ``` 1146: 1147: --- 1148: 1149: ### 14.4 Emergency Recovery Commands 1150: 1151: | Symptom | Recovery Command | 1152: | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | 1153: | MCP server hangs | `pkill -f "server-name" && opencode` | 1154: | Database corruption (Memory) | `rm -rf .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/` | 1155: | Config invalid JSON | Restore from backup or regenerate from Section 12 templates | 1156: | npm packages broken | `npm cache clean --force && npm install -g ` | 1157: | Python/uv issues | `uv cache clean && uv tool install --force` | 1158: | Skills not loading | Restart OpenCode. Verify SKILL.md frontmatter | 1159: | All else fails | Complete clean uninstall (14.2) then reinstall | 1160: 1161: --- 1162: 1163: ### 14.5 Health Check 1164: 1165: ```bash 1166: # Quick health check one-liner 1167: node -v && python3 -V && [ -f opencode.json ] && [ -d .opencode/skills ] && echo "✅ Core components OK" || echo "❌ Check failed" 1168: 1169: # Detailed checks 1170: ls .opencode/skills/ # Skills installed 1171: cat opencode.json | jq '.mcp | keys' # MCP servers configured 1172: ``` 1173: 1174: --- 1175: 1176: ### 14.6 Troubleshooting Matrix 1177: 1178: | Symptom | Likely Cause | Solution | 1179: | ----------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------- | 1180: | `Error: ENOENT opencode.json` | Config missing | Restore from backup or recreate | 1181: | `MCP server timeout` | Process stuck | Kill processes (14.4) | 1182: | `Database locked` | Multiple processes | Kill processes, restart | 1183: | `SQLITE_CORRUPT` | Database corruption | Delete and rebuild (14.4) | 1184: | `JSON parse error` | Invalid config | Validate with `jq`, fix syntax | 1185: | `Port already in use` | Port conflict | Kill conflicting process | 1186: | `Permission denied` | File permissions | Check ownership, run `chmod` | 1187: | `Memory not found` | DB not indexed | Run `memory_index_scan()` | 1188: 1189: --- 1190: 1191: ### 14.7 Best Practices 1192: 1193: 1. **Before changes:** Run backup command (14.1) 1194: 2. **After failure:** Run rollback (14.3), restart OpenCode 1195: 3. **Monthly:** Clean old backups: `ls -td ~/.opencode-backup-* | tail -n +6 | xargs rm -rf` 1196: 1197: --- 1198: 1199: 1200: 1201: ## 15. POST-INSTALLATION CONFIGURATION 1202: 1203: After installing OpenCode components, customize the AI agent configuration for your project. 1204: 1205: > **🎯 This is a public repo template.** Of the shipped skills, `sk-code` is the one that carries stack-specific patterns — start there when adapting to your project. The other shipped skills (`sk-doc`, `sk-git`, `sk-code-review`, `system-spec-kit`, the deep-research/deep-review loops, the `cli-*` orchestrators) are codebase-agnostic out of the box and work for any project regardless of frontend stack, animation library, CMS, or backend language. Many teams will also add their own skills on top of the shipped set — that's expected. See §15.5 below for the customization map. 1206: 1207: ### 15.1 AGENTS.md Customization 1208: 1209: The `AGENTS (Universal).md` file is a template for AI agent behavior. Customize it for your project: 1210: 1211: 1. **Rename the file**: `AGENTS (Universal).md` → `AGENTS.md` 1212: 2. **Choose project type**: Front-end, Back-end, or Full-stack 1213: 3. **Align with installed tools**: Update tool references to match your MCP configuration 1214: 4. **Align with available skills**: Update skills table to match `.opencode/skills/` 1215: 1216: **Quick customization for project types:** 1217: 1218: | Project Type | Primary Tools | Primary Skills | Remove/De-emphasize | 1219: | ------------ | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | 1220: | Front-end | Chrome DevTools, your CMS MCP | mcp-chrome-devtools, sk-code | Database tools, API patterns | 1221: | Back-end | API testing, Database tools | sk-code | Browser tools, your CMS MCP | 1222: | Full-stack | All tools | All skills | Nothing | 1223: 1224: **Detailed Guide**: [SET-UP - AGENTS.md](./SET-UP%20-%20AGENTS.md) 1225: 1226: ### 15.2 Skill Advisor Setup 1227: 1228: The Skill Advisor (`skill_advisor.py`) powers Gate 2 in AGENTS.md, routing requests to appropriate skills: 1229: 1230: ```bash 1231: # Verify skill advisor 1232: python .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py "help me write documentation" 1233: ``` 1234: 1235: If confidence > 0.8, the AI agent MUST use the recommended skill. 1236: 1237: **Detailed Guide**: [SET-UP - Skill Advisor.md](./SET-UP%20-%20Skill%20Advisor.md) 1238: 1239: ### 15.3 Skill Creation 1240: 1241: Create custom skills to extend AI agent capabilities: 1242: 1243: ```bash 1244: # Initialize new skill 1245: python .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/init_skill.py my-skill --path .opencode/skills 1246: 1247: # Validate skill 1248: python .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py .opencode/skills// 1249: ``` 1250: 1251: ### 15.4 Agent System 1252: 1253: The Agent System provides specialized AI personas with defined authorities, tool permissions and behavioral rules. Unlike skills (knowledge bundles), agents have **authority** to act and **tools** to execute. 1254: 1255: **Available Agents (10 total):** 1256: | Agent | Purpose | Key Capability | 1257: | --------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | 1258: | **context** | Context retrieval & analysis | Exploration dispatch, memory loading | 1259: | **debug** | Fresh-perspective debugging | Systematic 4-phase root cause analysis| 1260: | **deep-research** | Iterative research loops | Multi-round investigation and convergence | 1261: | **deep-review** | Iterative review loops | Findings-first release and quality audits | (Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1261. Use offset=1262 to continue.)
